text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence trained on enormous amounts of text data from books, websites, and articles. They learn patterns in language by predicting the next word in a sentence, which helps them generate human-like responses. To use an LLM, you give it a prompt—a question or instruction—and it produces an answer based on its training. This is called prompt engineering, where careful wording can improve results. LLMs are capable of writing essays, summarizing texts, and even creating code, but they have limits. They don't truly understand meaning; they can make mistakes, provide false information, and repeat biases found in their training data.
